headline: Corteva AGM: CEO touts 2025 gains, Q4 separation track, and Luke Kissam as new CEO
summary: Corteva (NYSE:CTVA) held its 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders virtually, with Chair Gregory Page opening the session and recognizing the company’s directors and leadership team. Page thanked directors Lamberto Andreotti and Michael Johanns, who he said will retire at the conclusion of the meeting

headline: Will Strong Quarter, Web3 Proxy Voting, and M&A Push Broadridge Financial Solutions' (BR) Narrative Forward?
summary: In late April 2026, Broadridge Financial Solutions reported quarterly results that exceeded analyst expectations, raised its full-year guidance, advanced Web3-enabled proxy voting through a partnership announced by Ondo Finance, and continued rolling out new trading and risk platforms alongside tuck-in acquisitions funded by strong free cash flow. Together, these moves highlight how Broadridge is using its balance sheet and technology stack to extend its role in both traditional and...
